original_thesis: The 'find the actual bug' stack. Step-back creates distance from the immediate problem; decomposition breaks it into tractable pieces; chain-of-thought traces the reasoning sequence; decision trees navigate branching possibilities; debugging strategies provide systematic elimination; systems thinking reveals context the parts miss. This stack prevents the most common failure mode in troubleshooting: fixing the wrong thing.
what_this_stack_defeats: Random debugging, premature diagnosis, fixing symptoms, tunnel vision on first hypothesis
notes: Thesis mentions step-back and systems-thinking which aren't members; actual members include formal-reasoning and critical-thinking instead. The core 'systematic elimination' thesis holds. Most applied/practical family in the set.
